     The Numbers Business Report

Midnight in Paris (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $56,816,662Details
International Box Office $105,235,313
Worldwide Box Office $162,051,975
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $15,479,383
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $5,054,099
Total $20,533,482
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A romantic comedy set in Paris about a family that goes there because of business, and two young people who are engaged to be married in the Fall have experiences there that change their lives. It's about a young man's great love for a city, Paris, and the illusion people have that a life different from theirs would be much better.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$599,003 (1.1% of total gross)
Legs:9.74 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:35.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$30,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:6 opening theaters/1,038 max. theaters, 10.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $85,688,209

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 20th, 2011 (Limited) by Sony Pictures Classics
International Releases: October 6th, 2011 (Wide), released as Полночь в Париже (Russia (CIS))
April 19th, 2012 (Wide) (Lebanon)
July 5th, 2012 (Wide) (South Korea)
Video Release: December 20th, 2011 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some sexual references and smoking.
(Rating bulletin 2164, 3/23/2011)
Running Time: 94 minutes
Award nomination: 2012 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Writers Guild of America Best Original Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Gratuitous Cameos
Plot point: Artists, In-Laws / Future In-Laws, Romance, Time Travel, Writing and Writers
Subgenre: Romantic Comedy
Time period setting: 1890s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Romantic Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Mediapro, Versatil Cinema, Gravier Productions, Pontchartrain
Production Countries: Spain, United States
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

A romantic comedy set in Paris about a family that goes there because of business, and two young people who are engaged to be married in the Fall have experiences there that change their lives. It's about a young man's great love for a city, Paris, and the illusion people have that a life different from theirs would be much better.
